sections.text:
The following data is from the Programme Educational Objectives table:
PEO1 indicates that graduates will be prepared for designing and developing efficient Information Technology systems and software with creativity and sustainability. PEO2 indicates that graduates will be skilled in the use of modern tools for critical problem solving and analyzing industrial and societal requirements. PEO3 indicates that graduates will be prepared with managerial and leadership skills for career and starting up own firms.

The following data is from the Programme Specific Outcomes table: PSO1 helps students apply the acquired knowledge and adapt modern tools to provide innovative solutions in Networking and Cyber Security applications.

The following data is from the Programme Specific Outcomes table: PSO2 enables students to design, implement and test mobile apps and software solutions for various automation applications.

The following data is from the Programme Outcomes (PO) — B.Tech Information Technology, MSAJCE table: PO1 Engineering Knowledge means to apply the knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals, and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems. PO2 Problem Analysis means to identify, formulate, review research literature, and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences, and engineering sciences. PO3 Design/Development Of Solutions means to design solutions for complex engineering problems and design system components or processes that meet the specified needs with appropriate consideration for the public health and safety, and the cultural, societal, and environmental considerations. PO4 Conduct Investigations of Complex Problems means to use research based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of the information to provide valid conclusions. PO5 Modern Tool Usage means to create, select, and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools including prediction and modeling to complex engineering activities with an understanding of the limitations. PO6 The Engineer and Society means to apply reasoning informed by the contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to the professional engineering practice. PO7 Environment and Sustainability means to understand the impact of the professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts, and demonstrate the knowledge of, and need for sustainable development. PO8 Ethics means to apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of the engineering practice. PO9 Individual and Team Work means to function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams, and in multi disciplinary settings. PO10 Communication means to communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large. PO11 Project Management and Finance means to demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the engineering and management principles and apply these to one's own work. PO12 Life long Learning means to recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life long learning in the broadest context of technological change.

The following data is from the Programme Outcomes table: PO1, Engineering Knowledge, requires students to apply mathematics, science, and engineering fundamentals to complex problems. PO2, Problem Analysis, enables students to identify, formulate, and analyze complex engineering problems. PO3, Design/Development, trains students to design solutions for complex engineering problems. PO4, Investigations, equips students to use research-based knowledge and methods for valid conclusions. PO5, Modern Tool Usage, prepares students to create and apply modern engineering and IT tools. PO6, Engineer and Society, allows students to assess societal, health, safety, legal, and cultural issues. PO7, Environment and Sustainability, enables students to understand the impact of engineering solutions on the environment. PO8, Ethics, requires students to apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics. PO9, Team Work, trains students to function as an individual and team member/leader. PO10, Communication, equips students to communicate effectively on complex engineering activities. PO11, Project Management, trains students to apply engineering and management principles. PO12, Life-long Learning, encourages students to engage in independent and life-long learning.

sections.header: 4. Job Profiles and IT Employment Forecast 2025–2030
sections.text: The following data is from the Placement Details of the Batch table: TCS recruited 483 students with a package of 3.6 LPA. Wipro recruited 356 students with a package of 3.5 LPA. Capgemini recruited 312 students with a package of 3.8 LPA. Infosys recruited 241 students with a package of 3.6 LPA. HCL recruited 145 students with a package of 4.25 LPA. Cognizant recruited 521 students with a package of 4 LPA. Accenture recruited 184 students with a package of 4.5 LPA. Tech Mahindra recruited 98 students with a package of 3.25 LPA. Emphasis recruited 64 students with a package of 4 LPA. IBM recruited 34 students with a package of 4.5 LPA.

Job opportunities are available in various MNCs in service and product companies as System Engineers, Software Professionals, Network Engineers, System Analysts, System Administrators, Project Leaders, Education sector, System Architects and Academicians. The Information Technology (IT) industry in India is poised for strong growth over the next five years, driven by domestic and global trends. Businesses across all sectors are adopting Cloud computing, AI, Big Data, IoT, and Blockchain, so demand for cloud engineers, data scientists, AI specialists, and DevOps engineers will rise. The Cloud market in India is growing rapidly, and AWS, Microsoft Azure, and Google Cloud will create high demand for cloud architects, cloud administrators, and cloud security specialists, and hybrid cloud solutions will generate opportunities in cloud migration, cloud operations, and cloud infrastructure management. AI is transforming healthcare, finance, manufacturing, and retail, and by 2030, demand will rise for data scientists, AI researchers, machine learning engineers, and AI-enabled software developers, requiring skills such as AI ethics, deep learning, and AI model deployment. With increasing cyberattacks and regulations (GDPR, Personal Data Protection Bill), demand for cybersecurity experts will grow, creating roles for ethical hackers, incident response professionals, security architects, and SOC analysts. India's cashless economy push requires blockchain developers, smart contract auditors, and DeFi specialists. The rollout of 5G will create demand for 5G network engineers, telecommunications architects, and telecom software developers, and IoT will see a boom in smart cities, agriculture, and healthcare. Government initiatives like Digital India, Smart Cities Mission, and Make in India will create roles for IT project managers, data engineers, IoT developers, and cloud solutions architects. India will continue as a global IT outsourcing hub with demand for application developers, IT consultants, systems integrators, and support specialists.

sections.header: --- 5. IT Domain Employment Opportunities and Salary Expectations
sections.text:
The following data is from the Employment Opportunities in IT Domain (2025–2030) section: Job roles in cloud computing and virtualization include Cloud Engineer, Cloud Solutions Architect, Cloud Security Specialist, DevOps Engineer, and Cloud Administrator. Required skills include AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Cloud security (IAM, encryption), Containers (Docker, Kubernetes), and Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Ansible). Relevant certifications are AWS Certified Solutions Architect, Microsoft Certified Azure Solutions Architect Expert, Google Cloud Professional Cloud Architect, and Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA). Job roles in artificial intelligence and machine learning include Data Scientist, AI Engineer, Machine Learning Engineer, NLP Specialist, and Deep Learning Engineer. Skills required are Python, R, Java; ML frameworks (TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch); Data visualization (Matplotlib, Seaborn); and knowledge of AI/ML algorithms and statistical models. Certifications include Microsoft Certified Azure AI Engineer Associate, Google Professional Machine Learning Engineer, IBM AI Engineering Professional Certificate, and AWS Certified Machine Learning – Specialty. Cyber Security job roles include Security Analyst, Ethical Hacker (Penetration Tester), Security Architect, SOC Analyst, and Cybersecurity Consultant. Skills include risk management, ethical hacking tools (Metasploit, Burp Suite), firewalls, SIEM tools (Splunk, LogRhythm), and incident response and forensics. Certifications are CEH, CISSP, CISM, CompTIA Security+, and CISA. Blockchain and Fintech job roles include Blockchain Developer, Smart Contract Developer, Blockchain Architect, Cryptocurrency Expert, and Blockchain Security Engineer. Skills required are Blockchain platforms (Ethereum, Hyperledger, Solana), smart contract development (Solidity, Rust), and DApp development. Certifications include Certified Blockchain Developer, Ethereum Developer Certification, and Certified Blockchain Professional (CBCP). IT Infrastructure and Networking job roles include Network Administrator, IT Systems Engineer, IT Infrastructure Manager, Virtualization Engineer, and Network Security Engineer. Skills include TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP, VMware, Hyper-V, VPN, IDS/IPS, and firewalls. Certifications are CCNA, CompTIA Network+, CISA, and VMware Certified Professional (VCP).

The following data is from the Expected Employment Growth Trends section: The Indian IT industry is expected to grow at an annual rate of 7-10%, especially in Cloud computing, Cyber security, AI, and data science. There is a job shift towards specialized IT roles, including Cloud engineers, AI specialists, Cyber security experts, and Data analysts. Cyber security, AI, data science, and Blockchain are expected to see the highest salary growth, with salaries 20-30% higher compared to other IT positions.

The following data is from the Salary Expectations in IT (India) — 2025 to 2030 section: Cloud Architects can expect Rs. 12 – Rs. 24 lakhs per annum. AI/ML Engineers can expect Rs. 8 – Rs. 20 lakhs per annum. Cybersecurity Experts can expect Rs. 7 – Rs. 15 lakhs per annum. Blockchain Developers can expect Rs. 10 – Rs. 25 lakhs per annum. Data Engineers can expect Rs. 8 – Rs. 18 lakhs per annum. IoT Engineers can expect Rs. 7 – Rs. 14 lakhs per annum. IT Project Managers can expect Rs. 15 – Rs. 25 lakhs per annum, depending on experience.

The following data is from the Salary Expectations in IT India 2025-2030 table: Cloud Architects can expect a salary range of Rs. 12 – Rs. 24 lakhs per annum. AI/ML Engineers can expect a salary range of Rs. 8 – Rs. 20 lakhs per annum. Cybersecurity Experts can expect a salary range of Rs. 7 – Rs. 15 lakhs per annum. Blockchain Developers can expect a salary range of Rs. 10 – Rs. 25 lakhs per annum. Data Engineers can expect a salary range of Rs. 8 – Rs. 18 lakhs per annum. IoT Engineers can expect a salary range of Rs. 7 – Rs. 14 lakhs per annum. IT Project Managers can expect a salary range of Rs. 15 – Rs. 25 lakhs per annum.
